title: Getting Started
description: A quick walkthrough of the core controls, tabs, and startup flow.

steps.title: Welcome
steps.body: This quick tour shows the main controls you need to get the app running. You can revisit this tutorial later from the Tutorials tab.

steps.title: Choose Avatar Engine
steps.body: Start here. Choose between VSeeFace and MuseTalk. MuseTalk is the advanced lip-sync avatar pipeline and the one most of the performance tuning is built around.

steps.title: Pick a Model
steps.body: Choose the LM Studio model here. Be sure LM Studio is running and serving a model before you initialize the system.

steps.title: Streaming and TTS
steps.body: These controls decide whether replies stream live and which text-to-speech backend is used. If you turn Stream Mode on, the app defaults to PocketTTS. If you turn Stream Mode off, it defaults back to the more expressive Chatterbox / TurboTTS path.

steps.title: MuseTalk VRAM Mode
steps.body: This chooses how demanding MuseTalk is on the GPU. Low VRAM modes make the system more accessible on smaller cards.

steps.title: Persona Tab
steps.body: The Persona tab controls voice, expressive tags, and the main system prompt. PocketTTS is bundled with the app and is used automatically when selected.

steps.title: Brain Tab
steps.body: The Brain tab controls model sampling behavior such as temperature and top-p. These shape how the assistant responds, but they are separate from performance tuning.

steps.title: Chunking Tab
steps.body: The Chunking tab controls how text is split for speech and avatar rendering. Most users should start with a profile or Dry Run before adjusting these manually.

steps.title: Dry Run and Profiles
steps.body: Dry Run measures your hardware and recommends better startup and chunking values. Performance Profiles let you save and reload good configurations later.

steps.title: Tutorials Tab
steps.body: This tab is where tutorials live. Over time, more walkthroughs can be added here without changing the app code because tutorials are loaded from JSON files.

steps.title: Initialize the System
steps.body: When LM Studio is ready and your settings look good, use this button to initialize the engine and start the companion system.
